Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add WIT namespace/name support

Introduce optional wit_namespace and wit_name for KnownPackage and store them on oci_repository. Adds schema columns and a migration, new upsert_with_wit paths that preserve existing values, and Store/RawKnownPackage plumbing to read/write these fields. Manager and indexer APIs now accept and propagate WIT mappings when indexing; registry file parsing populates PackageSource.namespace. Search was extended to first try an exact lookup by wit_namespace + wit_name and fall back to the previous repository LIKE behavior for compatibility. Tests and CLI snapshots were updated to include the new optional fields and an exact-lookup unit test was added.

* Preserve explicit WIT names in install flow

Track when the user provided a WIT-style name by extending install tuples to (Reference, update_manifest, Option<String>). Thread the explicit_name through the install pipeline and resolve function, set Some(name) for WIT-style inputs, and None otherwise. When present, use the explicit name as the manifest key instead of deriving from embedded metadata (which can be a placeholder); otherwise fall back to derive_component_name or the WIT package name. This prevents accidental placeholder names from being used as manifest keys.

---------

commit.message:
perf: match JSVG rendering defaults and expose renderingHint() API (#134)

* perf: remove VALUE_RENDER_QUALITY rendering hint

Remove the KEY_RENDERING = VALUE_RENDER_QUALITY hint from both
Surface.setupRenderingHints() and Defs pattern rendering. Java2D
defaults to VALUE_RENDER_DEFAULT which favors speed over quality.
This should improve rendering performance with minimal visual impact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add renderingHint() to ConversionBuilder API

Allow end users to override Java2D rendering hints via the builder:

  JairoSVG.builder()
      .fromBytes(svg)
      .renderingHint(RenderingHints.KEY_RENDERING,
                     RenderingHints.VALUE_RENDER_QUALITY)
      .toPng();

Defaults remain unchanged (antialias on, fractional metrics on,
stroke pure). User-specified hints are applied after defaults,
so they override any default value for the same key.

Added overloaded Surface.init() that accepts a Map of hint overrides.
Updated README with usage example.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: remove VALUE_FRACTIONALMETRICS_ON default

Drop KEY_FRACTIONALMETRICS hint from defaults. Users who need precise
floating-point text metrics can opt in via the renderingHint() API.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: match JSVG rendering hint defaults

Remove KEY_TEXT_ANTIALIASING from defaults. JairoSVG now uses the same
two rendering hints as JSVG out of the box:
- KEY_ANTIALIASING → VALUE_ANTIALIAS_ON
- KEY_STROKE_CONTROL → VALUE_STROKE_PURE

All other hints (KEY_RENDERING, KEY_TEXT_ANTIALIASING,
KEY_FRACTIONALMETRICS) use platform defaults, matching JSVG behavior.
Users can customize any hint via the renderingHint() builder API.

Updated comparison README to reflect matching defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update benchmark results with matching JSVG rendering defaults

Reran full benchmark suite now that JairoSVG uses the same rendering
hints as JSVG. Key highlights:
- JairoSVG and JSVG are nearly identical on most test cases
- 2–31× faster than EchoSVG, 1–2.5× faster than CairoSVG
- Gradients improved slightly (4.4ms vs previous 4.6ms)
- Patterns regressed to 6.1ms (median 4.9ms, p95 spike — likely GC)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: pre-cache SVG bytes and fix JSVG benchmark hints

- Pre-cache SVG content as byte[] in SvgCase record to avoid
  repeated String.getBytes() allocation on every iteration
- Update SvgConverter interface from String to byte[] input
- Fix JSVG benchmark rendering hints to match new JairoSVG defaults
  (removed VALUE_RENDER_QUALITY, TEXT_ANTIALIAS_ON, FRACTIONALMETRICS_ON)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(terrain): switch to binary .bin tiles (ArrayBuffer / Uint16)

Replaces PNG+canvas encoding with direct Uint16 binary files:
- tiling.js: writes tile_y_x.bin (Uint16 LE, 513x513 with overlap)
  - no Sharp dependency, much faster generation
- HeightmapDecoder.worker.js: fetch -> ArrayBuffer -> Uint16 -> Float32
  - no OffscreenCanvas, no endianness/channel-packing issues
- WorldManager.preloadHeightAt: same fetch+ArrayBuffer approach (async)
- WorldManager.getHeightAt: accounts for 513-wide tile stride
- WorldManager: loads tile_y_x.bin instead of .png
- Aethelgard.js: spawn offset 52 -> 100m for extra safety

commit.message:
fix(terrain): fix tile encoding - R=high byte, G=low byte (8-bit RGBA)

Browser OffscreenCanvas converts 16-bit grayscale PNG to R=G=B=high_byte,
discarding the low byte. HeightmapDecoder reads R/255+G/65025 expecting
R=high, G=low precision packing - this was causing massive spikes.

Fix: encode tiles as 8-bit RGBA with explicit R=high, G=low channel split.
